name: Minesweeper

window.title: Minesweeper
window._note: Matches any window with 'Minesweeper' in the title. Covers classic Minesweeper, Minesweeper X, and most clones. The Microsoft Store 'Microsoft Minesweeper' app has a modern UI that won't work with LCD OCR — use a classic clone instead.

_calibration_notes: Window targeting is enabled: vision captures only the Minesweeper window.
ROIs are RELATIVE to the window's top-left corner (0,0), not the full screen.
Classic Minesweeper layout (Expert board, default window size ~500x380):
- 8px border padding on left/right, ~3px on top (below title bar)
- Header bar starts at y≈55 (below title bar + menu bar)
- Mine counter: top-left of header, 3-digit red LCD, ~41x23px
- Timer:        top-right of header, 3-digit red LCD, ~41x23px
These ROIs are calibrated for classic Minesweeper at default Expert size.
If detection fails, recalibrate with: python tools/calibrate_rois.py --capture
The detector uses red-channel thresholding for LCD digits — only works on
classic-style 7-segment displays (bright red on dark background).

health._label: mines remaining counter — 3-digit red LCD at top-left of header bar

credits._label: timer seconds — 3-digit red LCD at top-right of header bar (Expert board width ~490px, counter at right edge minus padding)

phase._label: game state face button — centered in header bar (~half of Expert board width minus half face width)

output.message: 3 min in -- look for number patterns
